zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert De Bruce 1st Lord of Annandale 1st Lord Skelton於1062年出生在法國諾門迪時，他的父親Sir Robart or Robert De Bruce35歲，母親Emma De Brittany33歲。他和Agnes Waltheg至少有1個兒子。他於1100年移民到英國。他去世於1141年5月11日在英國英格蘭越可爾斯凱爾頓 卡素享年79歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bruce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from a place in Normandy, probably from Brix (La Manche).

History: The son of the Domesday baron, a friend of David I of Scotland, was granted by him the Lordship of Annandale in 1124, and his second son Robert became the founder of the Scottish house of Bruce. — This surname (see 2 above) is listed along with its original form Brousse in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Brousse
meanings: French: topographic name for someone living in a scrubby area, from Old French bro(u)ce ‘thicket, brushwood’ (from Late Latin bruscia), or a habitational name from any of several places in France named Brousse. Compare Bruce and Brusse .

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered form Bruce) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Brewis
meanings: (of Norman origin): habitational name from Briouze in Orne.
(Northumberland and Durham): topographic name or nickname from Middle English breuhous ‘brewery’ (literally ‘brew house’).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert De Bruce 1st Lord of Annandale 1st Lord Skelton was born in 1062, in Normandy, France, his father, Sir Robart or Robert De Bruce, was 35 and his mother, Emma De Brittany, was 33. He had at least 1 son with Agnes Waltheg. He immigrated to England in 1100. He died on 11 May 1141, in Skelton Castle, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Agnes Waltheg. Il a immigré au Angleterre en 1100. Il est décédé le 11 mai 1141, à Skelton Castle, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert De Bruce 1st Lord of Annandale 1st Lord Skeltonが1062年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた時，父Sir Robart or Robert De Bruceは35歳，母Emma De Brittanyは33歳であった。彼はAgnes Walthegとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1100年にイギリスに移住した。彼は1141年5月11日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアスケルトン カストルで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert De Bruce 1st Lord of Annandale 1st Lord Skelton(이)가 1062년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robart or Robert De Bruce(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma De Brittany(은)는 33세였다. 그는 Agnes Waltheg(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1100년에 영국로 이민을 떠났다. 그는 1141년 5월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 스켈턴 카스틀에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.
